headline_question: What is the final release-facing rule for strict deposit-component interpretation after the TOC/ROW incidence gates?

unresolved: Whether any future TOC incidence evidence justifies a validated strict share.
Whether the strict lane is still missing major direct creator channels beyond the current loan core.

takeaways: This summary freezes the release-facing rule after the incidence gates, not just the intermediate diagnostics.
Full TDC remains in the repo as the broad Treasury-attributed object, but TOC and ROW stay outside the strict object under current evidence.

The strict evidence hierarchy is now fixed around `strict_loan_core_min_qoq` as the headline direct benchmark and `strict_loan_core_plus_nonfinancial_corporate_qoq` as the standard bridge comparison; `strict_loan_core_plus_private_borrower_qoq` stays diagnostic.
The direct-core rule is horizon-specific rather than one-size-fits-all: multihorizon benchmark = `strict_loan_core_min_qoq`, impact-horizon candidate = `strict_loan_mortgages_qoq`.
The h0 release snapshot is now explicit: TOC/ROW support bundle ≈ 65.40, core residual ≈ -5.51, headline direct core ≈ -10.66, standard bridge comparison ≈ 9.44.

The raw incidence gate is what keeps the support bundle outside the strict object: TOC deposits-only share ≈ 0.66 versus reserves share ≈ 1.26, ROW-checkable share ≈ 0.02 versus foreign-NONTS share ≈ 2.26.
The narrow-TOC rescue branch is closed too: even the best candidate implies residual ≈ -34.16 with abs gap ≈ 23.50 to the headline direct core.
